name: Uptime Text Sensor
description: The `uptime` text sensor provides a human-readable representation the time since. The elements can be separated by a custom string, and more significant elements will be omitted if they are zero. For example, if the uptime is 1 day, 2 hours, 3 minutes, and 4 seconds, the sensor will report `1d2h3m4s` with the default configuration. By default leading zero elements will be trimmed from the output, e.g. if the uptime is 0 days, 0 hours, 3 minutes, and 0 seconds, the sensor will report `3m0s`. This can be disabled by setting `expand` to `true`.

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name for the sensor. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the sensor in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Device Class
config_entries.description: The device class for the sensor. Only the `timestamp` and `date` device classes are supported. Set to `""` to remove the default device class of a sensor. Requires Home Assistant 2024.3 or newer.

config_entries.label: Update Interval
config_entries.description: The sensor reporting interval. Defaults to `30s`.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Format
config_entries.description: Allows the customization of the output format. The following options are available:

config_entries.label: Days
config_entries.description: The string to use for the days element. Defaults to `d`.

config_entries.label: Expand
config_entries.description: If set, the text will always include all elements, even if they are zero. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Hours
config_entries.description: The string to use for the hours element. Defaults to `h`.

config_entries.label: Minutes
config_entries.description: The string to use for the minutes element. Defaults to `m`.

config_entries.label: Seconds
config_entries.description: The string to use for the seconds element. Defaults to `s`.

config_entries.label: Separator
config_entries.description: The separator to use between the uptime values. Defaults to the empty string.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.
